state.next-action: 44-04 and 44-05 shipped 2026-04-14. Dev-id copy/handoff tokens on the site now prefer source-searchable literals (`cid="..."`, `stableCid="..."`, `stableBandCid="..."`, `as="..."`) instead of runtime-only ids; `Identified`, `PageIdentified`, `SiteSection`, and the shared `DevIdAgentPromptDialog` all carry `componentTag` + `searchHint` through the same prompt path. `PageIdentified` now uses the shared band-panel path so page-level surfaces behave like nav/footer. `site/components/devid/DevPanel.tsx` is the single maintained panel surface: the compact panel now renders the "Dev Panel" header for both band and section modes, section scans no longer use the old long layout, and band panels use sticky in-band positioning instead of footer-style absolute placement. Skeptic route identifiers were normalized to explicit literals where needed (`cid="skeptic-hypotheses-site-section"`, `cid="skeptic-improvements-site-section"`) while rich component names like `as="SkepticHowUsedSection"` remain first-class and copy exactly as authored. Error captured in `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`: splitting the panel into divergent section-vs-band experiences let source-searchable ids regress and made the sticky UX inconsistent. Verification for this slice: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` still fails only on pre-existing unrelated errors in `app/gad/GadSkillBootstrapSection.tsx`, `app/security/SecurityOperationalStatusSection.tsx`, and generated `lib/catalog.generated.ts`. Next loop pick after this cleanup: either continue the dev-id rollout by replacing remaining auto-generated `SiteSection` bands with explicit `cid` literals on pages that still need greppable section shells, or return to the queued 44.5 species-editor work. 44-01, 44-02, 44-03 all shipped 2026-04-14. `gad eval verify` green: 1/1 runs fully preserved for app-forge v1. `/project-market` now lists 7 eval projects across 3 registered roots with live Launch eval bridge. 44-03: apps/forge/next.config.mjs gained `output: "export"` + `images.unoptimized`; removed `force-dynamic` from app/page.tsx and app/g/[project]/[species]/[version]/page.tsx; added generateStaticParams() to the dynamic route. `next build` produces 26 static pages including 23 generation routes under out/. bin/gad.cjs::evalPreserve now tries [out, dist, build] in order for static-servable build dirs and excludes out/.next/build from the source-tree copy. app-forge v1 build manually preserved to apps/portfolio/public/evals/app-forge/v1/ using the fresh forge out/ bundle; v1 TRACE.json gained runtime_identity.id="claude-code". Deleted v2/v3 leftover scaffolding from earlier 44-01 smoke tests. 44-02: multi-root eval discovery wired into build-site-data.mjs (previously hardcoded to the submodule's evals/); new helpers getEvalRoots() + listEvalProjectDirs() mirror bin/gad.cjs logic via bin/gad-config.cjs imported through createRequire. Top-level `evals/` dir registered as `[[evals.roots]]` id="portfolio-legacy" in gad-config.toml. 4 thin project overlays added (grime-time, repub-builder, mb-cli-framework, magicborn) with brownfield project.json + species/gad/species.json (baseline: HEAD, startsFromScratch: false). Site rebuild now shows 11 species / 7 projects (was 6/2). Skipped "global" as meta-not-launchable. 44-01 promoted from stub to live child_process bridge 2026-04-14. `site/app/api/dev/launch-eval/route.ts` now runs `node bin/gad.cjs eval run --project --execute` via `node:child_process.spawn` from the repo root and streams stdout/stderr/exit back as SSE. `ProjectCard.tsx` parses the event-stream, renders stdout (green) / stderr (red) / meta (grey) lines in a scrollable log panel under the card, tracks running/done/err states, and exposes an Abort button that signals the server via AbortController → SIGTERM to the child. NODE_ENV=development gate enforced at both route and button level; prod cannot render or hit it. Strict projectId/species validation (`/^[a-zA-Z0-9_\-./]+$/`, <128 chars) guards against argv injection. Smoke-tested end-to-end on localhost:3000 against `__smoke_test__` (expected "project not found" error path) and `app-forge` (real CLI run → created v3 scaffolding with TRACE.json/EXEC.json/PROMPT.md). Surfaced + fixed `bin/gad.cjs` bug: commit 17043d7 restored eval_type/workflow fields in RUN.md template after 42.4-18 left stray `gadJson` references that crashed every `gad eval run` invocation. New follow-up tasks filed: 44-02 (migrate legacy planning roots grime-time/repub-builder/mb-cli-framework/magicborn/global into canonical eval-project shape per user alignment "every project IS an eval project"), 44-03 (decide app-forge static-export vs alt preservation path — confirmed `.next/` is correct for a Next.js app but forge has no `output: "export"` so nothing statically servable lands under apps/portfolio/public/evals/app-forge/; preservation audit still MISSING). 43-11 shipped earlier same day: phase 44.5 renamed "Brood Editor" → "Species Editor" with /project-editor + /species-editor routes reserved and Lovable/Bolt visualization scope embedded; phase 45 stripped of "v4 brood" phrasing. Next loop pick: 44-02 (migrate legacy roots — fastest win, makes the launcher actually cover the whole workspace) or 44-03 (decide app-forge preservation strategy — unblocks `gad eval verify` green). Phase 42.4 cleanup shipped end-to-end: snapshot lane leak fixed (42.4-04), command-skill/workflow normalization shipped (42.4-05), remaining canonical-skill dedupe + validator shipped (42.4-06), and proto-skill staging/install is now normalized so staged drafts live in `.planning/proto-skills/` with direct runtime install via `gad evolution install` before promotion (42.4-07, gad-183). Species-level `gad.json` → `species.json` rename completed (42.4-14): all 6 species files renamed in escape-the-dungeon + gad-explainer-video, build-site-data.mjs species scanner updated. `gad:eval-spawn` skill + slash command shipped (42.4-13): new canonical skill `skills/gad-eval-spawn/` wraps the existing `gad eval run --execute` EXEC.json spec, spawns a worktree-isolated `general-purpose` subagent with the hydrated prompt, writes a session marker under `.planning/.eval-runs/<runId>.json`, and preserves outputs via `gad eval preserve` before cleanup — plan/build seam per decision forge-09. Multi-root eval discovery shipped (42.4-12): `[[evals.roots]]` in `gad-config.toml` mirrors `[[planning.roots]]`; new helpers `getEvalRoots` / `listAllEvalProjects` / `resolveEvalProject` in `bin/gad.cjs` rewire every eval subcommand to walk the union of configured roots plus the implicit submodule default, with load-time duplicate-id detection; `apps/forge/evals/` registered as the first non-default root so forge can host its own evals outside the framework submodule. Forge proving-ground spec shipped (42.4-16): `apps/forge/.planning/plans/forge-proving-ground.md` defines PG-01..PG-09 observable pass criteria (build preserved, clean headless load, zero console.error, game root mounts, player renders, tilemap renders, input moves player, no unhandled rejections, probe runtime < 30s) with a 12-row historical comparison table retroactively scoring all ETD gad-species generations — 4 of 12 (v7/v8/v9/v10) have preserved builds, exactly 1 (v8) has an explicit SCORE.md "Build requirement gate PASSED" annotation, and PG-02..PG-08 are `?` across the board because no mechanical probe has ever run against an ETD build. DOG-02 in forge `REQUIREMENTS.xml` and phase 08 success criteria in forge `ROADMAP.xml` now cite the checklist. Project ⊇ Species inheritance contract shipped (42.4-15, decision gad-184): new `lib/eval-loader.cjs` exports `loadProject`/`loadSpecies`/`loadAllSpeciesRaw`/`loadResolvedSpecies`/`loadAllResolvedSpecies`/`mergeProjectSpecies`/`resolveInheritanceChain`; merge semantics are project defaults → `inherits_from` chain (deepest ancestor first) → target species with last-write-wins, arrays REPLACE (no concat), species-only fields (`inherits_from`, `dna`, species description) stay local and never back-propagate to project defaults, cycles throw with a clear error. Wired `bin/gad.cjs eval list` and `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` through the loader so CLI, site prebuild, and forge editor all see the same merged shape. Hermetic test suite at `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` green (7/7) covering (a) empty-species inheritance, (b) clean override, (c) species-only non-pollution, (d) chain resolution, (e) cycle detection, plus `loadAllResolvedSpecies` + `mergeProjectSpecies` purity spot checks. All five of the 42.4-12..16 framework gates blocking app-forge phase 01 are now landed; forge phase 01 can spawn. 42.4-10 sink MD→XML audit shipped: determination (C) — no existing command converts markdown planning prose into canonical XML. `sink compile` reads MD but passes through to MDX without structuring; `sink decompile` is misleadingly named and only creates empty XML shells when source is missing (never reads sink content); `migrate-schema` goes XML→MD, wrong direction. Test verified against a temp markdown-only planning root. Spec for new `gad planning hydrate` subcommand (with per-file parsers inverse to existing `*XmlToMd` functions in `lib/docs-compiler.cjs`) documented in `references/sink-md-xml-audit.md` — NOT implemented, follow-up task needed (~150 LOC). Also flagged: `sink decompile` should be renamed or doc-fixed (it does not decompile), and `sink compile` should warn when it falls back to MD pass-through since the readers can't query that content. Canonical planning-root shape pinned (42.4-09, decision gad-185): `vendor/get-anything-done/references/project-shape.md` is the normative contract defining the 4-file minimum (`STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`), the canonical optional file/dir set, the legacy/deprecated list (gad.json→species.json, *.md legacy scaffolds, `config.json`, project-local `skills/` staging), per-file minimum valid XML headers for all six scaffolds, and an audit matrix across all seven registered planning roots (7/7 canonical-minimum-clean; grime-time flagged for legacy project-local `skills/` staging dir; gad + global have legacy `.planning/config.json`; three "thin" roots — magicborn, mb-cli-framework, repub-builder — missing optional REQUIREMENTS/ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS, informational only). `bin/gad.cjs projects audit` (the "gad health" surface) now computes a per-root canonical-shape report with three additive checks (`canonical_minimum`, `canonical_optional`, `canonical_legacy`) and a summary block citing the references doc as source of truth. 42.4-08 shipped 2026-04-14: `gad projects init` now defaults to canonical XML and scaffolds the six-file set (4-file canonical minimum + REQUIREMENTS/ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS) per `references/project-shape.md` §5. Added `--format md` flag for legacy markdown path (still writes the three canonical XML ledgers so the resulting root is audit-clean), `--projectid` arg, pre-flight overwrite check with `--force` escape hatch, and a post-init next-steps hint pointing at `gad projects audit` / `gad discuss-phase` / `gad plan-phase`. Verified by temp-dir init (6 files written, all parse under fast-xml-parser, canonical_minimum 4/4), refused re-run without `--force`, clean overwrite with `--force`, and legacy `--format md` scaffold in a fresh dir. No new decision — gad-185 remains the authoritative contract. 42.4-11 shipped 2026-04-14: `references/sink-pipeline.md` lands as the coherence map over 42.4-10's per-command audit — 8 sections (purpose, XML-as-source-of-truth, three-registry table `[[planning.roots]]`/`[[docs.projects]]`/`[[evals.roots]]`, ASCII flow diagram flagging broken arrows, 19-row quick-reference "edit X → command Y → verify with Z" table including explicit NOT SUPPORTED rows for MD→XML and MDX round-trip, footgun list extending 42.4-10's with "site compile and sink compile are independent axes both reading .planning/" and "docs_sink is singular monorepo-wide", related-docs links, change log). Three skills (gad-new-project, gad-plan-phase, gad-execute-phase) got a short `<planning_state_surface>` block referencing the new doc; gad-new-project also now cites `references/project-shape.md` in its execution_context. No new decision, no CLI changes.
